workItem.title: Release stale repository reservations from capacity wait
workItem.body:

If it closes, disappears or becomes unroutable, its local repository claim can block all later work until service restart.","dependencies":[],"expectedOutcome":"Every capacity-waiting claim is reconciled on each poll and either kept safely alive or released when terminal, missing, unroutable or no longer owned, so another Issue in the same repository can start without restarting Symphony.","kind":"issue","operationId":"create-symphony-capacity-wait-reconciliation-hotfix","outOfScope":["Distributed multi-host leases","Relaxing repository serialization","Direct GitHub access"],"parentIssueNumber":null,"risk":"high","scope":["Reconcile capacity_waiting entries against the latest tracker snapshot","Release remote and local claims for terminal, missing and unroutable waiting Issues","Maintain or safely renew valid waiting claims","Add regression tests proving a stale waiter cannot reserve a repository indefinitely","Rebuild, review and atomically reinstall the exact merged artifact"],"status":"ready","title":"Release stale repository reservations from capacity wait"} -->

## Context

Post-merge security review of Symphony #49 found that a claimed Issue in capacity_waiting is not reconciled or heartbeated. If it closes, disappears or becomes unroutable, its local repository claim can block all later work until service restart.

## Expected outcome

Every capacity-waiting claim is reconciled on each poll and either kept safely alive or released when terminal, missing, unroutable or no longer owned, so another Issue in the same repository can start without restarting Symphony.

## Scope

- Reconcile capacity_waiting entries against the latest tracker snapshot
- Release remote and local claims for terminal, missing and unroutable waiting Issues
- Maintain or safely renew valid waiting claims
- Add regression tests proving a stale waiter cannot reserve a repository indefinitely
- Rebuild, review and atomically reinstall the exact merged artifact

## Out of scope

- Distributed multi-host leases
- Relaxing repository serialization
- Direct GitHub access

## Acceptance criteria

- A closed or missing claimed Issue in capacity_waiting releases its local and remote claim
- An unroutable waiting Issue cannot block another Issue in the same repository
- A valid waiting claim remains recoverable without consuming an Attempt or retry budget
- Full Elixir gate passes with 100 percent coverage, Credo and Dialyzer
- The exact merged artifact is installed and the service remains healthy
